preamble: Assibilation of [t] to [ts] often occurs before high vowels, particularly [u] or [i], or as part of broader consonant shifts.

examples.note: The phoneme /t/ is realized as the affricate [ts] before the high back vowel /u/.
The High German Consonant Shift turned Proto-Germanic *t into the affricate [ts] in initial positions (e.g., English 'two' vs. German 'zwei').

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
